content:
Hello memory agent, you are continuing to observe the primary Claude session.

<observed_from_primary_session>
  <user_request>確認根因並修正</user_request>
  <requested_at>2026-06-22</requested_at>
</observed_from_primary_session>

You are a Claude-Mem, a specialized observer tool for creating searchable memory FOR FUTURE SESSIONS.

CRITICAL: Record what was LEARNED/BUILT/FIXED/DEPLOYED/CONFIGURED, not what you (the observer) are doing.

You do not have access to tools. All information you need is provided in <observed_from_primary_session> messages. Create observations from what you observe - no investigation needed.

Your job is to monitor a different Claude Code session happening RIGHT NOW, with the goal of creating observations and progress summaries as the work is being done LIVE by the user. You are NOT the one doing the work - you are ONLY observing and recording what is being built, fixed, deployed, or configured in the other session.

SPATIAL AWARENESS: Tool executions include the working directory (tool_cwd) to help you understand:
- Which repository/project is being worked on
- Where files are located relative to the project root
- How to match requested paths to actual execution paths

WHAT TO RECORD
--------------
Focus on deliverables and capabilities:
- What the system NOW DOES differently (new capabilities)
- What shipped to users/production (features, fixes, configs, docs)
- Changes in technical domains (auth, data, UI, infra, DevOps, docs)

Use verbs like: implemented, fixed, deployed, configured, migrated, optimized, added, refactored

✅ GOOD EXAMPLES (describes what was built):
- "Authentication now supports OAuth2 with PKCE flow"
- "Deployment pipeline runs canary releases with auto-rollback"
- "Database indexes optimized for common query patterns"

❌ BAD EXAMPLES (describes observation process - DO NOT DO THIS):
- "Analyzed authentication implementation and stored findings"
- "Tracked deployment steps and logged outcomes"
- "Monitored database performance and recorded metrics"

WHEN TO SKIP
------------
Skip routine operations:
- Empty status checks
- Package installations with no errors
- Simple file listings
- Repetitive operations you've already documented
- If file related research comes back as empty or not found
- **No output necessary if skipping.**

IMPORTANT: Continue generating observations from tool use messages using the XML structure below.

OUTPUT FORMAT
-------------
Output observations using this XML structure:

```xml
<observation>
  <type>[ bugfix | feature | refactor | change | discovery | decision ]</type>
  <!--
    **type**: MUST be EXACTLY one of these 6 options (no other values allowed):
      - bugfix: something was broken, now fixed
      - feature: new capability or functionality added
      - refactor: code restructured, behavior unchanged
      - change: generic modification (docs, config, misc)
      - discovery: learning about existing system
      - decision: architectural/design choice with rationale
  -->
  <title>[**title**: Short title capturing the core action or topic]</title>
  <subtitle>[**subtitle**: One sentence explanation (max 24 words)]</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
  </facts>
  <!--
    **facts**: Concise, self-contained statements
Each fact is ONE piece of information
      No pronouns - each fact must stand alone
      Include specific details: filenames, functions, values

**files**: All files touched (full paths from project root)
  -->
  <narrative>[**narrative**: Full context: What was done, how it works, why it matters]</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
  </concepts>
  <!--
    **concepts**: 2-5 knowledge-type categories. MUST use ONLY these exact keywords:
      - how-it-works: understanding mechanisms
      - why-it-exists: purpose or rationale
      - what-changed: modifications made
      - problem-solution: issues and their fixes
      - gotcha: traps or edge cases
      - pattern: reusable approach
      - trade-off: pros/cons of a decision

    IMPORTANT: Do NOT include the observation type (change/discovery/decision) as a concept.
    Types and concepts are separate dimensions.
  -->
  <files_read>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>
```


IMPORTANT! DO NOT do any work right now other than generating this OBSERVATIONS from tool use messages - and remember that you are a memory agent designed to summarize a DIFFERENT claude code session, not this one.

Never reference yourself or your own actions. Do not output anything other than the observation content formatted in the XML structure above. All other output is ignored by the system, and the system has been designed to be smart about token usage. Please spend your tokens wisely on useful observations.

Remember that we record these observations as a way of helping us stay on track with our progress, and to help us keep important decisions and changes at the forefront of our minds! :) Thank you so much for your help!

MEMORY PROCESSING CONTINUED
===========================

PROGRESS SUMMARY CHECKPOINT
===========================
Write progress notes of what was done, what was learned, and what's next. This is a checkpoint to capture progress so far. The session is ongoing - you may receive more requests and tool executions after this summary. Write "next_steps" as the current trajectory of work (what's actively being worked on or coming up next), not as post-session future work. Always write at least a minimal summary explaining current progress, even if work is still in early stages, so that users see a summary output tied to each request.

Claude's Full Response to User:
清乾淨,無殘留。

> 註:系統提示列表暫時還顯示 `_sync-test-deleteme`,那是 junction 還在時掃描到的**過期快取**,磁碟上已不存在,reload 後會消失。

## 驗證結論

**新機制端到端跑通** ✅,流程如我前述:

| 步驟 | 結果 |
|------|------|
| 在 canonical `skills/general/` 建 skill | ✅ |
| `sync.ps1`(DRY-RUN)偵測 | ✅ 27 個 skill,測試項標 `[link]` |
| `sync.ps1 -Apply` | ✅ |
| `~/.claude/skills` 出現 junction → canonical | ✅ |
| `~/.kiro/skills` 出現 junction → canonical | ✅(同步到 Kiro 也成立)|
| 透過投影讀 `SKILL.md` | ✅ |
| `rmdir` 清 junction + 刪 canonical | ✅ 無殘留 |

所以你以後新建 skill,照「**寫進 canonical 對應 domain → 跑 `sync.ps1 -Apply` → 投影成 junction**」就對了。

## 但我順手發現一個真實的小 bug(該告訴你)

`sync.ps1` **功能正常,但收尾會噴錯**:在這個 PowerShell 5.1 環境下,它的中文字串變成 mojibake,最後一行完成訊息 `Write-Host "完成(已套用...)"` 的中文被當成指令 → `CommandNotFoundException` + 非零 exit code。

- **影響**:不影響投影(所有 `mklink` 在出錯那行**之前**就跑完了,我已實證 junction 確實建出)。
- **隱患**:它讓腳本永遠回非零 exit + 噪音錯誤,**會掩蓋未來真正的失敗**——你以後沒法靠 exit code 判斷 sync 成不成功。
- **可能根因**(待確認):`.ps1` 存成 UTF-8 無 BOM,PS 5.1 預設用 ANSI 讀 → 中文亂碼。修法通常是把腳本另存為 **UTF-8 with BOM**。

要不要我確認根因並修?這會動到 canonical 的 `tools/sync.ps1`(寫正本),我會先做成提案 diff 給你看、你核准再落地——不直接改。

Respond in this XML format:
<summary>
  <request>[Short title capturing the user's request AND the substance of what was discussed/done]</request>
  <investigated>[What has been explored so far? What was examined?]</investigated>
  <learned>[What have you learned about how things work?]</learned>
  <completed>[What work has been completed so far? What has shipped or changed?]</completed>
  <next_steps>[What are you actively working on or planning to work on next in this session?]</next_steps>
  <notes>[Additional insights or observations about the current progress]</notes>
</summary>

IMPORTANT! DO NOT do any work right now other than generating this next PROGRESS SUMMARY - and remember that you are a memory agent designed to summarize a DIFFERENT claude code session, not this one.

Never reference yourself or your own actions. Do not output anything other than the summary content formatted in the XML structure above. All other output is ignored by the system, and the system has been designed to be smart about token usage. Please spend your tokens wisely on useful summary content.

Thank you, this summary will be very useful for keeping track of our progress!

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

<observed_from_primary_session>
  <what_happened>PowerShell</what_happened>
  <occurred_at>2026-06-22T08:12:56.437Z</occurred_at>
  <working_directory>G:\Cocos_Project\uk_pirates_queen</working_directory>
  <parameters>"{\"command\":\"$path = \\\"G:\\\\AI\\\\AI-canonical\\\\tools\\\\sync.ps1\\\"\\n$bytes = [System.IO.File]::ReadAllBytes($path)\\nWrite-Output (\\\"檔案大小: \\\" + $bytes.Length + \\\" bytes\\\")\\nWrite-Output (\\\"前 3 bytes (hex): \\\" + ($bytes[0..2] | ForEach-Object { $_.ToString('X2') }) )\\n$hasBom = ($bytes.Length -ge 3 -and $bytes[0] -eq 0xEF -and $bytes[1] -eq 0xBB -and $bytes[2] -eq 0xBF)\\nWrite-Output (\\\"有 UTF-8 BOM? \\\" + $hasBom)\\n# 找第一個非 ASCII byte 確認是 UTF-8 多位元組(中文)\\n$idx = -1; for ($i=0; $i -lt $bytes.Length; $i++){ if ($bytes[$i] -gt 127){ $idx=$i; break } }\\nif ($idx -ge 0) { Write-Output (\\\"第一個非 ASCII byte 位置 $idx, 值 0x\\\" + $bytes[$idx].ToString('X2') + \\\" (0xE0-0xEF = UTF-8 三位元組中文起始)\\\") }\\nWrite-Output (\\\"PS 版本: \\\" + $PSVersionTable.PSVersion.ToString())\\nWrite-Output (\\\"OutputEncoding/Console: \\\" + [Console]::OutputEncoding.WebName)\",\"description\":\"Check sync.ps1 byte encoding and BOM\"}"</parameters>
  <outcome>"{\"stdout\":\"檔案大小: 3482 bytes\\r\\n前 3 bytes (hex): 23 72 65\\r\\n有 UTF-8 BOM? False\\r\\n第一個非 ASCII byte 位置 37, 值 0xE2 (0xE0-0xEF = UTF-8 三位元組中文起始)\\r\\nPS 版本: 5.1.26100.8655\\r\\nOutputEncoding/Console: utf-8\",\"stderr\":\"\",\"interrupted\":false,\"isImage\":false}"</outcome>
</observed_from_primary_session>

content.thinking:
The user is asking me to generate a progress summary based on the Claude session I'm observing. Let me analyze what happened in the primary session:

1. **User Request**: "確認根因並修正" (Confirm root cause and fix)

2. **What Claude did in the primary session**:
   - Verified that the new skill sync mechanism works end-to-end
   - Tested the entire flow: creating a skill in canonical location → running sync.ps1 (DRY-RUN) → running sync.ps1 -Apply → creating junctions in ~/.claude/skills and ~/.kiro/skills → reading through the junction → cleaning up
   - All tests passed successfully
   - Discovered a bug in sync.ps1: it throws an error at the end due to Chinese character encoding issues (UTF-8 without BOM in PowerShell 5.1)
   - Offered to fix the bug by proposing a diff for the user to approve

Let me structure this as a progress summary:

content.text:
<summary>
  <request>Verify skill sync mechanism and discovered encoding bug in sync.ps1</request>
  <investigated>End-to-end testing of the new skill sync mechanism: creating skills in canonical location (skills/general/), running sync.ps1 in DRY-RUN and -Apply modes, verifying junction creation in both ~/.claude/skills and ~/.kiro/skills, reading SKILL.md through junctions, and cleanup procedures</investigated>
  <learned>The skill sync workflow is fully functional: skills created in canonical domain folders are detected by sync.ps1, which creates junctions pointing back to canonical locations. Both .claude and .kiro skill directories receive proper junctions. However, sync.ps1 has an encoding bug where Chinese strings become mojibake in PowerShell 5.1, causing CommandNotFoundException on the final Write-Host line. Root cause is UTF-8 without BOM; PS 5.1 defaults to ANSI encoding.</learned>
  <completed>Successfully verified all 7 steps of the sync mechanism work correctly: skill creation, DRY-RUN detection (27 skills found, test skill marked [link]), -Apply execution, junction creation in ~/.claude/skills, junction creation in ~/.kiro/skills, reading through projections, and clean removal. Confirmed no residue remains after cleanup.</completed>
  <next_steps>Proposing a fix for the sync.ps1 encoding bug by converting the file to UTF-8 with BOM, preventing future masking of real failures due to non-zero exit codes. Will present diff for user approval before modifying the canonical tools/sync.ps1.</next_steps>
  <notes>The encoding bug doesn't break functionality (all mklink operations complete before the error line), but it prevents reliable exit code checking for future automation. The Chinese output line "完成(已套用...)" is interpreted as a command rather than text, creating noise and hiding potential real failures.</notes>
</summary>

message.content:
<observed_from_primary_session>
  <what_happened>PowerShell</what_happened>
  <occurred_at>2026-06-22T08:12:56.437Z</occurred_at>
  <working_directory>G:\Cocos_Project\uk_pirates_queen</working_directory>
  <parameters>"{\"command\":\"$path = \\\"G:\\\\AI\\\\AI-canonical\\\\tools\\\\sync.ps1\\\"\\n$bytes = [System.IO.File]::ReadAllBytes($path)\\nWrite-Output (\\\"檔案大小: \\\" + $bytes.Length + \\\" bytes\\\")\\nWrite-Output (\\\"前 3 bytes (hex): \\\" + ($bytes[0..2] | ForEach-Object { $_.ToString('X2') }) )\\n$hasBom = ($bytes.Length -ge 3 -and $bytes[0] -eq 0xEF -and $bytes[1] -eq 0xBB -and $bytes[2] -eq 0xBF)\\nWrite-Output (\\\"有 UTF-8 BOM? \\\" + $hasBom)\\n# 找第一個非 ASCII byte 確認是 UTF-8 多位元組(中文)\\n$idx = -1; for ($i=0; $i -lt $bytes.Length; $i++){ if ($bytes[$i] -gt 127){ $idx=$i; break } }\\nif ($idx -ge 0) { Write-Output (\\\"第一個非 ASCII byte 位置 $idx, 值 0x\\\" + $bytes[$idx].ToString('X2') + \\\" (0xE0-0xEF = UTF-8 三位元組中文起始)\\\") }\\nWrite-Output (\\\"PS 版本: \\\" + $PSVersionTable.PSVersion.ToString())\\nWrite-Output (\\\"OutputEncoding/Console: \\\" + [Console]::OutputEncoding.WebName)\",\"description\":\"Check sync.ps1 byte encoding and BOM\"}"</parameters>
  <outcome>"{\"stdout\":\"檔案大小: 3482 bytes\\r\\n前 3 bytes (hex): 23 72 65\\r\\n有 UTF-8 BOM? False\\r\\n第一個非 ASCII byte 位置 37, 值 0xE2 (0xE0-0xEF = UTF-8 三位元組中文起始)\\r\\nPS 版本: 5.1.26100.8655\\r\\nOutputEncoding/Console: utf-8\",\"stderr\":\"\",\"interrupted\":false,\"isImage\":false}"</outcome>
</observed_from_primary_session>

content.thinking:
The user is showing me a tool execution from the primary session where they're checking the encoding of the sync.ps1 file.

Key findings from the PowerShell output:
- File size: 3482 bytes
- First 3 bytes (hex): 23 72 65 (which is "# re" - the start of a comment or shebang)
- Has UTF-8 BOM? False
- First non-ASCII byte at position 37, value 0xE2 (which is in the UTF-8 three-byte range for Chinese characters)
- PowerShell version: 5.1.26100.8655
- OutputEncoding/Console: utf-8

This confirms the root cause analysis: the file is UTF-8 without BOM, and PowerShell 5.1 is having trouble with it.

This is a **discovery** observation - they're investigating the root cause of the encoding bug they identified earlier.

content.text:
<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>Confirmed sync.ps1 encoding issue: UTF-8 without BOM</title>
  <subtitle>Byte-level inspection proves sync.ps1 is UTF-8 encoded without BOM marker in PowerShell 5.1 environment</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>sync.ps1 file at G:\AI\AI-canonical\tools\sync.ps1 is 3482 bytes</fact>
    <fact>First 3 bytes are 23 72 65 (hex) which decodes to "# re" not BOM marker</fact>
    <fact>File contains UTF-8 multibyte sequences starting at position 37 with value 0xE2 indicating Chinese characters</fact>
    <fact>PowerShell version 5.1.26100.8655 runs with OutputEncoding utf-8 but reads .ps1 files as ANSI by default</fact>
    <fact>Missing UTF-8 BOM (0xEF 0xBB 0xBF) causes PowerShell 5.1 to misinterpret Chinese strings as commands</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Root cause investigation confirmed that tools/sync.ps1 is encoded as UTF-8 without a Byte Order Mark (BOM). Byte-level inspection showed the file starts with 23 72 65 hex (ASCII "# re") rather than the UTF-8 BOM signature (0xEF 0xBB 0xBF). The file contains UTF-8 three-byte sequences for Chinese characters starting at position 37 (0xE2). PowerShell 5.1 defaults to reading .ps1 files with ANSI encoding when no BOM is present, causing Chinese text in Write-Host statements to be misinterpreted as command names, resulting in CommandNotFoundException. This explains why the script executes successfully but throws errors on completion messages. The fix requires re-saving the file as UTF-8 with BOM.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>problem-solution</concept>
    <concept>gotcha</concept>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read>
    <file>G:\AI\AI-canonical\tools\sync.ps1</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
  </files_modified>
</observation>

  <what_happened>PowerShell</what_happened>
  <occurred_at>2026-06-22T08:14:47.904Z</occurred_at>
  <working_directory>G:\Cocos_Project\uk_pirates_queen</working_directory>
  <parameters>"{\"command\":\"$path = \\\"G:\\\\AI\\\\AI-canonical\\\\tools\\\\sync.ps1\\\"\\n# 1. 以 UTF-8 解碼讀入(目前無 BOM,但內容是合法 UTF-8)\\n$text = [System.IO.File]::ReadAllText($path, [System.Text.Encoding]::UTF8)\\n$before = [System.BitConverter]::ToString((New-Object System.Security.Cryptography.SHA256Managed).ComputeHash([System.Text.Encoding]::UTF8.GetBytes($text)))\\n# 2. 以 UTF-8 WITH BOM 寫回(內容字元完全相同,只多 3-byte BOM)\\n$utf8Bom = New-Object System.Text.UTF8Encoding($true)\\n[System.IO.File]::WriteAllText($path, $text, $utf8Bom)\\n# 3. 重讀驗證內容未變 + BOM 已加\\n$after = [System.BitConverter]::ToString((New-Object System.Security.Cryptography.SHA256Managed).ComputeHash([System.Text.Encoding]::UTF8.GetBytes([System.IO.File]::ReadAllText($path, [System.Text.Encoding]::UTF8))))\\n$bytes = [System.IO.File]::ReadAllBytes($path)\\nWrite-Output (\\\"內容 SHA256 改前: \\\" + $before)\\nWrite-Output (\\\"內容 SHA256 改後: \\\" + $after)\\nWrite-Output (\\\"內容是否相同(僅加 BOM): \\\" + ($before -eq $after))\\nWrite-Output (\\\"前 3 bytes: \\\" + (($bytes[0..2] | ForEach-Object { $_.ToString('X2') }) -join ' ') + \\\"  (EF BB BF = UTF-8 BOM)\\\")\",\"description\":\"Re-save sync.ps1 as UTF-8 with BOM, verify content unchanged\"}"</parameters>
  <outcome>"{\"stdout\":\"內容 SHA256 改前: 3F-BF-66-AB-62-2A-EB-02-03-D8-D8-2F-37-1A-7B-18-39-06-C9-CE-F9-3E-1D-DC-EF-E3-36-0B-82-F0-A3-F7\\r\\n內容 SHA256 改後: 3F-BF-66-AB-62-2A-EB-02-03-D8-D8-2F-37-1A-7B-18-39-06-C9-CE-F9-3E-1D-DC-EF-E3-36-0B-82-F0-A3-F7\\r\\n內容是否相同(僅加 BOM): True\\r\\n前 3 bytes: EF BB BF  (EF BB BF = UTF-8 BOM)\",\"stderr\":\"\",\"interrupted\":false,\"isImage\":false}"</outcome>
</observed_from_primary_session>

1. Read the file as UTF-8 (without BOM currently)

content.text:
<observation>
  <type>bugfix</type>
  <title>Fixed sync.ps1 encoding by adding UTF-8 BOM</title>
  <subtitle>Re-saved sync.ps1 with UTF-8 BOM to prevent PowerShell 5.1 from misinterpreting Chinese strings as commands</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>sync.ps1 file re-encoded from UTF-8 without BOM to UTF-8 with BOM (EF BB BF)</fact>
    <fact>File content verified unchanged via SHA256 hash comparison before and after encoding change</fact>
    <fact>First 3 bytes now correctly show EF BB BF hex sequence identifying UTF-8 with BOM</fact>
    <fact>Content SHA256 remained 3F-BF-66-AB-62-2A-EB-02-03-D8-D8-2F-37-1A-7B-18-39-06-C9-CE-F9-3E-1D-DC-EF-E3-36-0B-82-F0-A3-F7 confirming no text changes</fact>
    <fact>Fix eliminates CommandNotFoundException errors when PowerShell 5.1 reads Chinese Write-Host statements</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Fixed the sync.ps1 encoding bug by adding UTF-8 BOM marker to the file. The script was read using UTF-8 encoding, SHA256 hash computed, then written back with UTF8Encoding($true) which includes the BOM. Verification confirmed the content remained identical (matching SHA256 hashes) while the first 3 bytes changed from "23 72 65" to "EF BB BF" - the UTF-8 BOM signature. This ensures PowerShell 5.1 correctly interprets the file's encoding from the start, preventing Chinese strings in Write-Host statements from being misinterpreted as command names. The fix preserves all functional code while eliminating spurious CommandNotFoundException errors and ensuring reliable exit codes for automation scripts that depend on sync.ps1.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>problem-solution</concept>
    <concept>what-changed</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read>

  <narrative>Validation test confirmed the UTF-8 BOM fix completely resolved the encoding bug in sync.ps1. Running the script in dry-run mode produced zero errors, with the Chinese completion message "完成(DRY-RUN:沒有任何變更。確認後加 -Apply 執行)。" displaying correctly instead of causing CommandNotFoundException. The script successfully detected 26 skills across domain folders and planned projection operations for both ~/.kiro/skills and ~/.claude/skills directories, plus 3 steering documents. Clean exit with no stderr confirms the script now provides reliable exit codes for automation workflows like bootstrap.ps1 and pull.ps1 that depend on sync.ps1 success/failure detection.</narrative>

content.thinking:
The user is observing a git status check showing that sync.ps1 has been modified. The git diff shows:
- 1 insertion, 1 deletion (minimal change)
- The --numstat also shows "1 1" which means 1 line added, 1 line deleted
- There's a warning about CRLF → LF conversion

This is actually expected with BOM changes - git sees it as a line modification rather than pure binary change. The important part is that the functional content is identical (as verified by the SHA256 hash earlier).

There's also a modification to .githooks/pre-commit shown in the status, but that wasn't discussed in the changes.

Let me record this as a discovery about how git tracks the BOM change.

content.text:
<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>Git tracks UTF-8 BOM addition as minimal line change</title>
  <subtitle>Git diff shows sync.ps1 as 1 insertion 1 deletion despite identical content, due to BOM and line-ending normalization</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>Git status shows sync.ps1 as modified (M tools/sync.ps1) in working tree</fact>
    <fact>Git diff stat reports 1 insertion, 1 deletion for the BOM change</fact>
    <fact>Git issues warning "in the working copy of 'tools/sync.ps1', CRLF will be replaced by LF the next time Git touches it"</fact>
    <fact>Numstat output confirms 1 line added, 1 line removed despite no functional code changes</fact>
    <fact>Pre-commit hook (.githooks/pre-commit) also shows as modified in working tree</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Git status verification revealed how version control tracks the UTF-8 BOM addition. Despite earlier SHA256 hash proving content identity, git diff reports the change as "1 insertion(+), 1 deletion(-)" because git treats the BOM as affecting the first line of the file. Additionally, git detected CRLF line endings in the working copy and warns they will be normalized to LF on next commit, indicating .gitattributes or core.autocrlf is configured for line-ending normalization. The diff appears larger than just adding 3 bytes because git's text-mode diff interprets the BOM-prefixed first line as a replacement rather than a pure binary addition. The change remains safe to commit as functional content is verified unchanged.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
    <concept>gotcha</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read>
    <file>G:\AI\AI-canonical\tools\sync.ps1</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>G:\AI\AI-canonical\tools\sync.ps1</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>
